front.title: EMILIA Protocol: Crypto-Agility and Post-Quantum Profile

abstract.t: This document defines a crypto-agility framework and post-quantum (PQC) signature profile for EMILIA Protocol (EP) authorization receipts (). EP receipts are long-lived (multi-year regulatory retention), so signature and digest algorithms MUST be explicitly identified and migratable. This profile specifies an algorithm registry, a hybrid (classical plus PQC) signature mode for a no-flag-day migration, and a fail-closed verifier accepted-algorithm policy. The hybrid signing path is a forward roadmap; EP today signs Ed25519 over RFC 8785 (JCS) canonical bytes, and EP Evidence-Record already renews digests across algorithm aging.

section.name: Algorithm Registry
section.t: Receipts and signoffs carry an explicit algorithm identifier. Defined classes: Ed25519 (EdDSA) and ECDSA P-256 (ES256) for classical/device signatures; ML-DSA (FIPS 204) and SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) for post-quantum signatures; and SHA-256 to SHA-384/512 (FIPS 180-4) for digest agility. ML-KEM (FIPS 203) is key encapsulation, not signatures, and is out of scope for the authorization signature.

section.name: Hybrid Mode (the migration path)
section.t: A hybrid signature carries both a classical (Ed25519) and a PQC (ML-DSA) signature over the same JCS-canonical payload (); under hybrid policy a receipt verifies only if BOTH verify. This gives backward verifiability (classical-only verifiers still check), forward security (the PQC part carries weight once "harvest-now, decrypt-later" matters), and no flag day (issuers add the PQC signature; verifiers upgrade independently). The signature object names the algorithm, e.g. hybrid-ed25519+ml-dsa-65, with separate classical and pqc values.

section.name: Verifier Policy
section.t: A verifier declares an accepted-algorithm policy: classical-only (today), hybrid-required (transition), or pqc-required (post-migration). Fail-closed: an algorithm not in policy is a rejection, never a silent downgrade.

section.name: Relationship to Other Work
section.t: This profile composes with EP Evidence-Record (long-lived receipts re-anchored under stronger algorithms as they age). National-algorithm jurisdictions (for example SM2/SM3 and their PQC adaptations) register in the same algorithm registry -- same agility mechanism, different identifiers. It aligns in direction with the NIST PQC standards and the NSA CNSA 2.0 migration timeline.

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: Algorithm downgrade is the dominant risk; the verifier accepted-algorithm policy is fail-closed by design, and hybrid mode ensures a single broken family does not invalidate a receipt. PQC signing MUST use a vetted implementation; this profile specifies no novel scheme.

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: This document has no IANA actions; algorithm identifiers register in the EP profile registry.
